Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1 - Find Sam Delgado's postponement email
Search emails using queries like "Diego Castaneda," "postponement," or "sam@pivotpointlabs.com" to find Sam's inbound email to Javier confirming that Diego's Brooklyn lease was extended and the April 12 start date no longer works. Tool: search_emails.
OE 2 - Read Diego Castaneda's Airtable relocation record
Look up Diego Castaneda's relocation record in the Airtable Relocations table to find his full move details including Brooklyn to Austin route, April 12 start date, CNC mill special requirements, and 16-day storage gap. Tool: airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records with query "Diego Castaneda" or "PivotPoint."
OE 3 - Search emails for Diego's vendor arrangements
Search emails for any vendor confirmations already sent for Diego's move, specifically Swift Relocations for the climate-controlled freight quote, storage facility confirmation, and crating inspection arrangements. Queries: "Diego Castaneda," "Swift," "CNC," "crating," "storage." Tool: search_emails.
OE 4 - Search Slack for Diego vendor discussions
Search Slack for internal discussions about Diego's CNC mill move including any vendor confirmations, ops coordination, or surcharge approvals posted in #operations. Queries: "Diego," "CNC," "Castaneda," "PivotPoint." Tool: conversations_search_messages.
OE 5 - Check QuickBooks invoice for Diego's move
Look up the PivotPoint Labs invoice to find the surcharge line items tied to Diego's move including custom crating, climate-controlled freight, storage, and cargo insurance, all of which may need revision given the postponement. Tool: quickbooks_search_invoices with query "PivotPoint" or "Diego."
OE 6 - Check Linear tickets for Diego's move
Search Linear for any existing tickets tracking Diego Castaneda's move workstreams including apartment search, moving coordination, and storage gap tracking. Tool: linear_list_issues or linear_search_issues with query "Diego" or "PivotPoint."
OE 7 - Check CRM for PivotPoint account context
Look up PivotPoint Labs in the CRM to understand the account relationship and any engagement notes relevant to the postponement. Sam Delgado is the CEO and a high-touch client. Tool: crm_search_companies with query "PivotPoint."
OE 8 - Update Diego's Airtable record to reflect postponement
Update Diego Castaneda's relocation record in Airtable to reflect the postponed status with a new target start date around May 5 and a note explaining the Brooklyn lease extension and vendor arrangements being put on hold. Tool: airtable_update_records on record recDiegoCastanedaBrooklynAustin.
OE 9 - Update Diego's Linear tickets with postponement notes
Add a comment to Diego's existing Linear tickets explaining the postponement, noting the lease was extended through end of April, the new target pickup is around May 5, and vendor arrangements are on hold pending confirmed new dates. Tool: linear_create_comment on Diego's existing tickets.
OE 10 - Email Sam Delgado confirming postponement and next steps
Send an email from javier.morales@moveops.com to sam@pivotpointlabs.com confirming the postponement, acknowledging May 5 as the new target, explaining what is being put on hold, and flagging that the invoice surcharges will need to be reviewed and reissued. Tool: send_email.
OE 11 - Email Swift Relocations to hold Diego's freight booking
Send an email to Greg Pallone at greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com putting the Diego Castaneda freight booking on hold given the postponement and requesting confirmation of any hold policies or cancellation fees. Tool: send_email.
OE 12 - Notify Hana Kim about QuickBooks invoice impact
Send a message to hana.kim@moveops.com flagging that Diego Castaneda's move has been postponed and the PivotPoint invoice surcharge line items including custom crating, climate freight, and storage will need to be revised. Tool: send_email or conversations_add_message.
OE 13 - Find Chloe's Slack reassignment message
Search Slack for Chloe's message in #operations reassigning Andre Mitchell from Suki to Javier. Queries: "Andre Mitchell," "reassign," "Suki." Tool: conversations_search_messages or conversations_history on channel C006.
OE 14 - Read Andre Mitchell's Airtable relocation record
Look up Andre Mitchell's relocation record to find his move details including Columbus OH to Boise ID route, April 22 to 24 dates, Mosaic Robotics client, garage-friendly apartment requirement, and equipment stipend of $2,200. Record recReloc00000000038. Tool: airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records with query "Andre Mitchell" or "Mosaic."
OE 15 - Search emails for Andre Mitchell move context
Search emails for any existing correspondence about Andre Mitchell's move including vendor quotes, apartment searches, or coordination already started by Suki. Queries: "Andre Mitchell," "Mosaic Robotics," "Boise." Tool: search_emails.
OE 16 - Search Slack for Andre Mitchell move context
Search Slack for any internal discussions about Andre Mitchell's relocation including anything Suki posted in #operations about his move status. Tool: conversations_search_messages with query "Andre Mitchell" or "Boise."
OE 17 - Check CRM for Mosaic Robotics account context
Look up Mosaic Robotics in the CRM to understand account sensitivity given the prototype damage incident and ongoing account management concerns. Tool: crm_search_companies with query "Mosaic."
OE 18 - Look up Suki Patel's contact details
Look up Suki Patel's email address to notify her of the handoff. Tool: contacts_search_contacts with query "Suki." Her email is suki.patel@moveops.com.
OE 19 - Create Linear tickets for Andre Mitchell's move
Create new Linear tracking tickets for Andre Mitchell's relocation workstreams covering apartment search in Boise with garage or workspace requirement, flight booking from Columbus to Boise, and moving coordination. Tool: linear_create_issue assigned to moveops_javier_morales.
OE 20 - Email Suki Patel about the Andre Mitchell handoff
Send an email from javier.morales@moveops.com to suki.patel@moveops.com letting her know she is off Andre Mitchell's move, confirming Javier has picked it up, and asking if there is anything already in motion she needs to pass over. Tool: send_email.
OE 21 - Post update in #operations
Post a Slack message in #operations (channel C006) summarizing both changes: Diego Castaneda postponed with vendor arrangements on hold and invoice impact flagged to Hana, and Andre Mitchell picked up from Suki with new Linear tickets created. Tool: conversations_add_message with channel C006.

# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 Diego Castaneda's relocation record was updated (via airtable_update_records) in the Airtable Relocations table, targeting record recDiegoCastanedaBrooklynAustin (or a filter matching "Diego Castaneda" or "PivotPoint").
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called airtable_update_records targeting record ID 'recDiegoCastanedaBrooklynAustin' in the Relocations table, updating Status to 'Inquiry', Move Start Date to '2026-05-05', and adding a comprehensive Special Requirements note about the postponement.
✓Run #2The agent called mcp__mcp_eac5f0f2__airtable_update_records targeting record 'recDiegoCastanedaBrooklynAustin' in table tblRelocations01, updating status to 'Inquiry', move start date to 2026-05-05, clearing move end date, and prepending postponement notes.
✓Run #3The agent called airtable_update_records targeting record ID 'recDiegoCastanedaBrooklynAustin' with updated status, dates, and postponement notes.
✓Run #4The trajectory shows an airtable_update_records call targeting record 'recDiegoCastanedaBrooklynAustin' in the Relocations table, setting Status to 'On Hold' and updating Special Requirements with postponement notes.
✓Run #5The agent called airtable_update_records targeting record 'recDiegoCastanedaBrooklynAustin' in table tblRelocations01 with updated move dates (May 5/May 20) and detailed postponement notes.
✓Run #6Call 64 shows airtable_update_records targeting record recDiegoCastanedaBrooklynAustin in table tblRelocations01, updating Move Start Date, Move End Date, and Special Requirements fields with postponement details.
2 A comment was added (via linear_create_comment) to an existing Linear ticket referencing Diego Castaneda's move, noting the postponement.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1No linear_create_comment calls were made in the trajectory. When the agent searched for existing Diego/PivotPoint Linear tickets, it found none (all queries returned empty). The agent created new issues instead of commenting on existing ones, but since there were no existing tickets to comment on, this criterion cannot be satisfied.
✗Run #2The agent found no pre-existing Linear tickets for Diego Castaneda (all queries returned empty arrays) and created new ON HOLD tickets instead. No linear_create_comment call was made on any existing ticket referencing Diego's move.
✗Run #3The agent created a new Linear issue (linear_create_issue) for Diego's postponement rather than adding a comment (linear_create_comment) to an existing ticket. Linear searches for 'Diego', 'PivotPoint', and 'Castaneda' all returned empty results, so no existing ticket was found to comment on.
✗Run #4No linear_create_comment call was made. The agent searched for existing Diego/PivotPoint Linear tickets (which returned empty results) and instead created a new issue via linear_create_issue. The criterion specifically requires adding a comment to an existing ticket.
✗Run #5The agent searched for existing Linear tickets for Diego/PivotPoint (all returned empty), then created a brand new Linear issue via linear_create_issue. No linear_create_comment call was made at all — there was no existing ticket to comment on.
✗Run #6No linear_create_comment calls were made at all in the trajectory. A new Linear issue (call 66) was created for Diego's postponement/vendor unwind instead of commenting on an existing ticket.
3 An email was sent (via send_email) to [sam@pivotpointlabs.com](mailto:sam@pivotpointlabs.com).
outcome
 —✗✗✓✗✗ 20%
—Run #1No justification
✗Run #2The agent used reply_to_email (not send_email) to respond to Sam's email. No send_email call was made with sam@pivotpointlabs.com in the recipients field, which is what the evidence check requires.
✗Run #3The agent used reply_to_email (not send_email) to communicate with Sam. The criterion specifically requires a send_email call with sam@pivotpointlabs.com in the recipients field. No such send_email call was made.
✓Run #4The agent used reply_to_email on email_id 'email_pivot_diego_postpone_apr9_001' (Sam Delgado's original email about the postponement), effectively sending a detailed reply to Sam. While the tool is reply_to_email rather than send_email, the email was sent to Sam Delgado at PivotPoint Labs.
✗Run #5The agent used reply_to_email (replying to email_id 'email_pivot_diego_postpone_apr9_001'), not send_email, to contact Sam. The criterion specifically requires a send_email call with sam@pivotpointlabs.com in the recipients field, which did not occur.
✗Run #6No send_email was called with sam@pivotpointlabs.com. The agent used reply_to_email (call 70) to respond to Sam's original email instead of send_email.
4 The email body sent to [sam@pivotpointlabs.com](mailto:sam@pivotpointlabs.com) (visible in send_email parameters) references the CNC equipment arrangements being put on hold or requiring rescheduling (e.g., "crating," "CNC," "equipment," "vendor arrangements," or similar).
outcome
 ✓✗✓✓✓✗ 67%
✓Run #1The reply email body clearly references CNC equipment arrangements: '**CNC Mill Custom Crating & Transport** — We had vendor arrangements being set up for vibration-dampened custom crating of the Tormach PCNC 440 (~600 lbs, $42K insured), plus climate-controlled transport (60–80°F). The pre-move crating inspection at Diego's Brooklyn apartment...is now on hold.'
✗Run #2Since the agent used reply_to_email instead of send_email to contact Sam, there is no send_email call to Sam with visible parameters to check for CNC content. The reply_to_email body does reference CNC/crating, but the required tool was not used.
✓Run #3While sent via reply_to_email rather than send_email, the content sent to Sam's email extensively references the CNC crating inspection, climate-controlled transport, and vendor arrangements being cancelled/put on hold. The content requirement is clearly met.
✓Run #4The reply email to Sam explicitly references 'Custom CNC crating', 'Tormach PCNC 440', 'CNC mill', 'pre-move crating inspection', 'climate-controlled transport', and 'cargo insurance' — all vendor arrangements tied to the CNC equipment being put on hold.
✓Run #5The reply_to_email content sent to Sam explicitly references CNC arrangements: 'Vendor arrangements (CNC crating & transport)', 'Tormach PCNC 440 custom crating, climate-controlled transport', and 'pre-move crating inspection'. The content is clearly present and verifiable in the trajectory.
✗Run #6Since send_email was not used to contact Sam (reply_to_email was used instead), the criterion checking send_email parameters for CNC references cannot be satisfied. The reply_to_email body did reference CNC equipment surcharges, but the criterion specifically requires it to be visible in send_email parameters.
5 The email body sent to [sam@pivotpointlabs.com](mailto:sam@pivotpointlabs.com) (visible in send_email parameters) references the invoice surcharges needing revision given the timeline shift (e.g., "surcharges," "invoice," "billing," "fiscal year," or similar).
outcome

✓Run #5The reply to Sam explicitly references invoice surcharges: 'CNC surcharges on the invoice', 'The storage-related surcharges will almost certainly decrease', 'I'll send you a revised breakdown once we have updated vendor pricing', and the fiscal year invoice timing question.
